title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Bioluminescence is the production and emission of light (0) by a living organism, a phenomenon most common in the deep ocean. (9) Although we often associate light with heat, this 'cold light' is remarkably efficient, losing very little energy. (10) Such is the darkness of the midnight zone that over ninety percent of creatures there have evolved some form of light. (11) Provided that an organism can produce its own glow, it can use it to attract prey or communicate with mates. (12) Not only do deep-sea fish use light for hunting, but they also employ it as a clever defense mechanism against predators. (13) Regardless of the depth at which they live, these species have adapted to survive in extreme pressure and near-freezing temperatures. (14) Not until scientists developed specialized submersibles did they realize how prevalent this ability actually was in the marine world. (15) Since the first high-definition cameras reached the ocean floor, we have discovered thousands of bioluminescent species previously unknown to science. (16) Whether these light patterns are used for camouflage or social signaling is a topic of ongoing research. This radiant adaptation highlights the incredible diversity of life found in the Earth's least explored and most mysterious environments.

example.text: Bioluminescence is the production and emission of light (0) by a living organism.
example.explanation: Preposition used to indicate the agent performing the action.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast.
Determiner used for emphasis, leading to 'that' in the following clause.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' structure.
Part of the fixed phrase 'Regardless of'.
Part of the emphatic 'Not until' structure requiring inversion ('did they realize').
Conjunction indicating the starting point of a period of time.
Conjunction used to introduce two alternatives.
